questions.description: An Armstrong number (narcissistic number) is a number equal to the sum of its digits each raised to the power of the number of digits.
questions.formula: Let d = number of digits. Armstrong if: n = sum(digit^d for each digit of n)

questions.description: Find the Greatest Common Divisor of two numbers.
questions.formula: Euclidean Algorithm: GCD(a, b) = GCD(b, a mod b). Base case: GCD(a, 0) = a

questions.description: Find the Least Common Multiple of two numbers.
questions.formula: LCM(a, b) = (a * b) / GCD(a, b). Use Euclidean algorithm for GCD first.

questions.description: Find the sum of all even Fibonacci numbers up to (and including) n.
questions.formula: Generate Fibonacci sequence. Sum only even-valued terms where term <= n.

questions.description: A Perfect Number equals the sum of all its proper divisors (excluding itself).
questions.formula: Sum all d where 1 <= d < n and n % d == 0. If sum == n, it is Perfect.

questions.description: Find all Armstrong numbers in the given range [l, r].
questions.formula: For each n in [l,r]: let d = number of digits. If sum(digit^d for each digit) == n, it is Armstrong.

questions.description: A Strong Number is one where the sum of factorial of each digit equals the number itself.
questions.formula: sum(factorial(digit) for each digit d of n) == n

questions.description: Decode a run-length encoded string where each character is followed by its count.
questions.formula: For each (char, count) pair in encoded string: output = char repeated count times. Concatenate all.

hidden_tests.input: one two three four five six

questions.description: An Automorphic number is one whose square ends with the number itself.
questions.formula: Compute square = n * n. Check if str(square).endswith(str(n)).

questions.description: Find the area of a circle given its radius.
questions.formula: Area = PI * r * r. Use PI = 3.14159265358979. Round to 2 decimal places.

visible_tests.input: the sky is blue and clear

questions.description: Print all Fibonacci numbers less than or equal to n.
questions.formula: F(0)=0, F(1)=1, F(n)=F(n-1)+F(n-2). Generate terms while term <= n.

questions.description: A Happy Number reaches 1 when repeatedly replaced by the sum of squares of its digits. Otherwise it is Not Happy.
questions.formula: Repeat: n = sum(digit^2 for each digit of n). If n == 1 → Happy. Detect cycle using a visited set → Not Happy.

questions.formula: Let d = number of digits. Armstrong if: n = sum(digit^d for each digit of n)
